item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: http://stackandhustle.com.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Terms and Conditions
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
1. Introduction

This website is operated by Stack and Hustle Ltd. Throughout the site, the terms “we,” “us,” and “our” refer to Stack and Hustle. We provide this website, including all information, tools, and services, conditioned upon your acceptance of these Terms and Conditions. By visiting our site, registering for an event, making a donation, purchasing a course, or engaging with our services, you agree to be bound by these Terms.

2. Eligibility

You must be at least 18 years old to use our services. If you are under 18, you may only use the site with parental or guardian consent. By using this website, you confirm that all information you provide is true, accurate, and current.

3. Use of Our Website and Services

You agree to use our website and services in accordance with applicable laws. You must not engage in fraudulent, abusive, or unlawful activity. You must not reproduce, duplicate, copy, sell, resell, or exploit any part of the site or services without express written permission. You must not interfere with the operation or security of the website. We reserve the right to refuse service to anyone, for any reason, at any time.

4. Intellectual Property Rights

All content on this site, including text, images, graphics, logos, videos, and software, is the property of Stack and Hustle Ltd unless otherwise stated. You may not copy, reproduce, distribute, or create derivative works from our content without prior written permission. You are granted a limited license to access and make personal use of our site but not to download, modify, or exploit it commercially.

5. Payments and Refunds

All payments made for courses, events, or services are processed securely. Once payment is confirmed, access to the service or course is granted. Unless otherwise stated in the specific course or event terms, all payments are non-refundable. Exceptions may be considered in cases of duplicate transactions or proven technical errors.

6. Donations

When making a donation through our website, you confirm that you are authorized to use the payment method. You understand that donations are voluntary and generally non-refundable. You authorize us to process your contribution for the purposes stated on the donation page.

7. Limitation of Liability

While we aim to provide accurate and up-to-date information, we do not guarantee that our website or services will be error-free, uninterrupted, or secure. Stack and Hustle Ltd will not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. We will not be liable for loss of data, revenue, profits, or business opportunities. We will not be liable for damages resulting from reliance on any information provided on the site.

8. Third-Party Links

Our website may include links to third-party websites. These links are provided for your convenience only. We do not endorse or control these sites. We are not responsible for their content, policies, or practices. We recommend reviewing the terms and privacy policies of any third-party site you visit.

9. Privacy and Data Protection

Your use of this site is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which explains how we collect, use, and protect your personal information. By using our services, you consent to the collection and use of your data as outlined in the Privacy Policy.

10. Suspension and Termination

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account or restrict access to our services if you breach these Terms and Conditions, engage in fraudulent, abusive, or unlawful activity, or cause harm to other users, the community, or our reputation.

11. Governing Law

These Terms and Conditions shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any disputes arising shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in FCT Abuja, Nigeria.

12. Changes to Terms and Conditions

We may update these Terms from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with the updated date. Continued use of the website after changes means you accept the new terms. It is your responsibility to review these Terms regularly.

item.title: Book an Appointment
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded: [bookingpress_form]

item.title: My Bookings
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded: <!-- wp:shortcode -->[bookingpress_my_appointments]<!-- /wp:shortcode -->

item.title: Thank You!
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
Payment Details
🏦 Bank Name: PAYSTACK-TITAN
💳 Account Name: IFMEDIALTD / STACK AND HUSTLE
🔢 Account Number: 9839239954

You can chat with us or just confirm your payment through WhatsApp during business hours at 0810 837 3034.

Booking ID: [booking_id]

We have sent your booking information to the email address you provided.

Service:
[bookingpress_appointment_service]

Date & Time:
[bookingpress_appointment_datetime]

Customer Name:
[bookingpress_appointment_customername]

Add to Calendar
[bookingpress_appointment_calendar_integration]

⚠️ Heads up: Nearby construction

There is ongoing construction work in the next building, and you may notice occasional noise during your session. We’re doing our best to keep things comfortable — please consider coming with earphones/earbuds or headphones if you’re sensitive to sound.

If you’d like help choosing a quieter time slot, message us on WhatsApp: 0810 837 3034.

Thank You for Choosing Stack & Hustle CoWorking Space
Powered by IF Media Ltd

If you’ve already made your payment, your booking is confirmed - we look forward to hosting you.
If you have not yet made a payment, please complete a bank transfer at least 24 hours before your booking to secure your space.
Please note: Bookings not paid on time will be made available to the next person.

item.title: Appointment canceled
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
Sorry to hear that you have requested for cancel the appointment.
We have sent an email notification for cancel appointment. So please click on the button to cancel the appointment.

item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
(25 customer reviews)

There are many variations of passages of Lorem Ipsum and available, but the majority have suffered alteration in somey form, by injected humour, or randomised words which don't look even slightly believable./p>

  • Going through the cites of the word in classical.
  • There are many variations of passages.
  • Making it look like readable and spoken English.

item.title: SIP & MINGLE
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded:
🚀 SIP & MINGLE: TECH EDITION 🍷💻
Small chops, wine, and speed networking - all in one vibrant evening at Stack&Hustle Coworking Space!

Join innovators, developers, designers, and entrepreneurs for an unforgettable night of connection, collaboration, and tech vibes. Come expand your network, share ideas, and be part of Abuja’s growing tech community! 🌍✨

📅 Saturday, Oct 18th, 2025
🕕 6 PM – 9 PM
📍 Vault Mall, Plot 436 Ali Muhammad Zarah St, CBD Abuja
🎟️ Free Entry — RSVP Required
👉 Click the link in our bio to RSVP!
🎙️ Meet Founder & Host - @ifeanyioluoha

(25 customer reviews)

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Mass comment blasting: $10 for 100k comments. All from unique blog domains, zero duplicates. I will provide a full report and guarantee Ahrefs picks them up. Email mailto:helloboy1979@gmail.com for payment info.If you received this, you know Ive got the skills.

item.title: UI/UX DESIGN CONTEST
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
Think you can design the next great mobile experience?
Join the Stack & Hustle UI/UX Design Contest - a 4-hour virtual challenge where designers across Nigeria compete to create stunning mobile app screens.

Win ₦150,000, land a remote foreign job opportunity, and have your portfolio showcased across major platforms.

This year, we’re proud to have Azumini Homes as our official partner - Nigeria’s modern real estate platform redefining how people buy, rent, and experience property through innovation and design.

📅 Sunday, Oct 26th | 4PM - 9:30PM
📍 Compete Virtual or live in Abuja At Stack&Hustle
🎟 Register Free: Click the link in our bio or visit stackandhustle.ng

item.title: GAME NIGHT: TECH EDITION I
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
🎮 Game Night: Tech Edition I ✨

Get ready for an evening of fun, friendly competition, and powerful connections! 🚀
Join us at Stack & Hustle for a vibrant night where tech professionals, entrepreneurs, and creatives come together to unwind, play, and network in style.

💡 Expect laughter, light snacks, and engaging games - all in Abuja’s most dynamic hub for developers, designers, and innovators.

📍 Venue: Stack & Hustle, Plot 436, Ali Muhammad Zarah St., CBD, Abuja
📅 Date: Sat, Nov 15th
🕕 Time: 6 PM - 9 PM
💰 Entry: ₦5,000 (limited slots)

🎟️ RSVP via the link in our bio! Access is limited to verified guests only.

item.title: WATCH NIGHT
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
🔥 STACK & HUSTLE x NACALI PRESENT 🔥
A FREE PREMIER LEAGUE WATCH NIGHT!

Arsenal vs Spurs - WHO’S TAKING WIN? ⚽🔥
Pull up this Sunday, November 23rd, as we turn Stack & Hustle into the loudest football hub in Abuja!
FREE ENTRY • LIMITED SPOTS • DOORS OPEN AT 3 PM

Come rep your team, rock your jerseys, bring the energy, and enjoy the match with Abuja’s most vibrant tech + sports community.
Plus 👉 FIFA gameplay available 👀🎮

📍 Venue: Stack & Hustle
436 Ali Muhammad Zarah Street, CBD, Abuja
🎟️ Grab your FREE ticket at stackandhustle.ng

Don’t dull - seats will go FAST! ⚡🔥

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: The Ace365app is a really practical choice if you like mobile app gaming. Try ace365app, you might like it.

item.title: Adams Ohindasi Wisdom #30
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
This artwork shows the quiet, strong resilience of a street entrepreneur. The rain stands for unexpected challenges like changes in the economy, weather, or social problems,that can affect a person’s work.
The main figure focuses not on herself, but on protecting her goods. The umbrella acts like an extra arm, showing the extra effort needed to keep the business safe when times are hard. This piece honors those who keep standing strong, protecting their work, and making sure that even in a storm, their business and dreams stay safe.

item.title: Alfred Paul #50
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: This artwork was created as my official entry for the Stack & Hustle Abuja Graphic Designers and Illustrators Contest, under the Rendering category. From the very beginning, my intention was not just to make something visually striking, but to make a statement about where art has come from, where it is now, and where it’s going. My core motivation was to bridge three worlds: The discipline and visual language of the Old Masters, particularly the Renaissance era Modern digital technology and 3D rendering workflows An African perspective, both in form, identity, and narrative weight I deliberately drew inspiration from Renaissance compositions the dramatic poses, divine symbolism, sculptural anatomy, and painterly lighting seen in classical works. However, instead of recreating them traditionally, I reinterpreted those ideas through modern rendering technology, using 3D as my canvas. At the same time, I infused the piece with an African outlook. The subject’s features, skin tone, strength, and presence were intentional. This was important to me; placing African identity confidently within a visual space that has historically excluded it, especially in classical “divine” or mythic representations. From a technical standpoint, the artwork was also a direct response to the fundamentals of strong rendering: • Lighting: Carefully controlled to echo Renaissance chiaroscuro while remaining physically believable in a 3D environment. • Textures: Skin, fabric, metal, and clouds were treated with realism and subtle imperfection to avoid a synthetic feel. • Reflections: Used selectively to enhance material definition without overpowering the composition. • Volumetrics: Clouds, atmosphere, and light rays were layered to create depth, scale, and a sense of transcendence. Every element was intentional. The angels, clouds, crown, and environment all serve the idea of divinity, burden, power, and reflection, echoing classical themes while speaking in a modern visual language.

item.title: Oji KingDavid Uchechukwu – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: This design was based on the current situation of bad governance we are facing in Africa, and the possibility of a better outcome in the future if the people unite

item.title: Victor Elera – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: Reimagining how culture is preserved in the age of AI

item.title: Timothy Sanga – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: Just had a fire vibe and decided to share it

item.title: Farida Mohammed – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: My design shows how AI can create any musical culture

item.title: Biyoo Davies – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: A group of African children — boys and girls of varying ages — standing together side by side at golden hour. Each child wears an ornate crown on their head, handcrafted from natural precious resources: gleaming gold filigree, polished silver leaves, raw uncut diamonds catching the light, deep blue sapphires, and emerald accents woven with natural vines and wildflowers. The children are gazing forward toward a vast, luminous horizon where warm golden sunlight breaks through dramatic clouds, casting volumetric god rays across a lush green African landscape. The atmosphere is filled with soft dust particles and pollen floating in the golden light. The scene is set amidst the remnants of chaos — scattered debris and worn structures fade into the background — but the children stand tall, unbothered, radiating hope, triumph, and quiet freedom.

item.title: Anas Bello – AI Submission
item.dc:creator: support_if-media
item.content:encoded: My Design serves a prove of concept to show upcoming artists who are on tight budget that they can still come up with great visuals to market their music without spending too much and also give them a fair ground by leveraging AI as music business currently is very expensive to venture into this serves a better alternative and also make their videos ready to compete with other artist with bigger budget, This is very good opportunity for artists to put out more videos

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: My Design serves a prove of concept to show upcoming artists who are on tight budget that they can still come up with great visuals to market their music without spending too much and also give them a fair ground by leveraging AI as music business currently is very expensive to venture into this serves a better alternative and also make their videos ready to compete with other artist with bigger budget, This is very good opportunity for artists to put out more videos

My Design serves a prove of concept to show upcoming artists who are on tight budget that they can still come up with great visuals to market their music without spending too much and also give them a fair ground by leveraging AI as music business currently is very expensive to venture into this serves a better alternative and also make their videos ready to compete with other artist with bigger budget, This is very good opportunity for artists to put out more videos

My Design serves a prove of concept to show upcoming artists who are on tight budget that they can still come up with great visuals to market their music without spending too much and also give them a fair ground by leveraging AI as music business currently is very expensive to venture into this serves a better alternative and also make their videos ready to compete with other artist with bigger budget, This is very good opportunity for artists to put out more videos

item.title: Biyoo Alexandre Davies #33
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: The name of my motion illustration is Mountain Boy: A modern boy connected to his ancestral roots. His forefathers' final stronghold during a conflict was the mountain they battled to defend. Learning to dance, he dances on the mountains whenever he has the opportunity to hike in his new surroundings.

item.title: Chidubem Ekere #68
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
In any digital canvas, everything begins with a grid. The lines stretch across the space like invisible rules guiding, measuring, holding every element in place. At first, it looks simple: a tree, a monument, a mosque in the distance, and a lone warrior standing tall. But like every story of a nation, nothing here exists on just one layer. The tree grows from the soil, its roots buried deep in history. It represents the land itself, ancient, patient, and alive. Seasons may change, storms may come, yet it stands, reaching upward. Behind it rises the mosque, a symbol of faith and culture. Its domes and towers remind the people that belief and tradition shape the spirit of a nation. At the center stands a monument, solid and deliberate. It marks the struggles and victories carved into the country's past moments that built the foundation of today. And then there is the warrior. He is not just a man but a representation of strength, resilience, and the enduring will of the people. His stance is calm but powerful, as if guarding everything behind him: the land, the culture, the memories. The grid holds them all together.
Each object exists on its own layer, separate yet connected. Just like Nigeria itself; different histories, beliefs, and people stacked together to form one complex picture. Remove one layer, and the story changes. Keep them all, and the image becomes whole. Through structure and balance, the message remains clear: Nigeria is built on many layers, but together they stand strong.
Stand strong, Naija!

item.title: David Bradford #48
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: This design blends human + interface + code, which is the basics of a frontend theme. It comprises a human figure (developer/designer) interacting with a floating UI, but their body is partially composed of glowing code, representing the fusion of human creativity and frontend technology.

item.title: David Nwoko #34
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
My artwork tells a story about unity among Nigeria’s diverse cultures.
The inspiration behind this design comes from the idea that Nigeria’s strength lies in its diversity.
It draws from the rich cultural heritage of Nigeria where different ethnic groups, traditions, and symbols coexist. Instead of showing them separately, the artwork brings them together in one central composition to emphasize unity over division.
At the center, the map of Nigeria is overlaid with the national flag colors, symbolizing one nation. The traditional staffs and royal items represent the major ethnic groups (like Hausa, Igbo, and Yoruba), each with its own heritage, authority, and identity, putting them together shows that despite cultural differences, the nation stands stronger together. The drums and patterns around the edges highlight celebration, rhythm, and cultural richness.
The bold “NIGERIA” and the phrase “Unity in Diversity” reinforce the message: many cultures, one people, united as a nation. NIGERIA, WE HAIL!!!

item.title: Destiny Remy-Chukwunyere #12
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
In many African societies, identity is shaped by a mix of tradition, colonial history, and modern influence. People often feel pressured to present versions of themselves that fit expectations, creating “masks” that don’t always reflect who they truly are.
In this design, the mask represents those imposed or performed identities, while the figure beneath reveals the authentic self. Removing the mask symbolizes reclaiming identity and breaking free from external definitions.

item.title: Emmanuel Ekwunuga #39
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
My branding project is centered around a fictional creative hub called “STACK,” designed to represent the growth and evolution of young creatives in Abuja.
The concept behind the brand is inspired by the idea of “stacking” skills — where individuals combine multiple talents such as design, video editing, and digital media to create sustainable opportunities for themselves.
The visual identity is clean and structured, reflecting clarity, growth, and intentional development. The use of bold typography and minimal color emphasizes professionalism, while the modular layout system represents flexibility and collaboration within the creative space.
Through this brand, I aimed to communicate that success in today’s creative industry is not accidental, but built step by step — by stacking knowledge, skills, and experiences over time.
“STACK” is more than a brand; it is a representation of a movement — where creativity meets structure, and hustle becomes growth.

item.title: Eshi Obute #44
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
The design depicts the general consensus of AI, that is slowly becoming a reality for many creatives. The consensus is "AI ID RIDDING US OF OUR ABILITY TO CREATE".
I highlighted four important things: 1. A brain belonging to a creative. The brain is active and bright, still firing as it should. 2. The dependence on AI, shown in the dark, glitchy background. 3. Loss of creative autonomy, depicted by the hand over the brain, representing an inability to function creatively, and a reliance on the quickness of AI tools etc. 4. The unseen damage, represented by the red streaks of lightning that are concentrated in the middle of the brain and appear to be subtly splitting it in two, showing a complete destruction of human creativity.
The design shows the gradual decay of a creative who is overly dependent on the quick fix AI provides, which leads to a loss of creative identity.

item.title: Freeman Lucky #70
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
My design represents my life recently, particularly the past few weeks since I came to Abuja for my NYSC service year.
Coming here felt like stepping onto a new road, but what I quickly realized was that it wasn’t just one road. It was many. Different paths appeared almost immediately, each one leading to a different possible destination. Opportunities, uncertainties, responsibilities, and ambitions all started to intersect at the same point in my life. And at the center of that intersection is me.
Standing at this crossroads sometimes feels strange. It's almost like watching my own life from the outside, as if there’s a fourth wall separating me from the moment. I can see the possibilities in front of me, the different directions I could take, yet I sometimes wonder: am I truly moving forward, or am I just standing still in the middle of it all?
There are days I question myself deeply. What if this moment is my own version of a rat race? What if I’m sitting at the center of these roads simply thinking about the journeys I could take, instead of actually stepping into one and moving?
Sometimes the quiet voice in my head says, “just manage”, and then the phrase “Yakubu, manage!” echoes in my mind; a reminder of how easily people can settle, adapt, and continue moving even when things feel uncertain.
But beneath all those thoughts lies the biggest question I’ve been asking myself lately: "What am I doing right now?"
This design captures that feeling; the crossroads, the stillness in the middle, and the quiet tension between uncertainty and possibility. It reflects a period of reflection, where the paths ahead are visible, but the courage to choose and move forward is still being discovered.

item.title: Kingsley Odeh #65
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
My design explores the concept of scalability as a journey of intentional growth, both in life and in business. Scalability is not just about expansion; it is about the ability to grow progressively while building stronger foundations at every stage. This artwork illustrates that growth happens step by step, through deliberate effort, motivation, and the continuous development of personal values.
At the base of the illustration is the silhouette of a person at ground level, representing the beginning of every journey. Everyone starts from a point of uncertainty, where the future is not yet defined. This stage symbolizes the moment when an individual decides to pursue progress and embrace the possibility of growth.
The ascending colorful walls or steps represent different stages of development. Each step is intentionally colored to reflect the emotional and psychological qualities required to move upward. The journey begins with yellow, symbolizing happiness, optimism, attention, and warmth. These qualities represent the first spark of motivation that encourages someone to take the first step toward their goals.
As the journey continues, the steps transition through colors that symbolize important personal and professional attributes. Orange reflects energy, enthusiasm, and friendliness, which help build momentum. Green represents growth, health, prosperity, and connection with nature, symbolizing stability and steady development. Purple conveys creativity, wisdom, and royalty, highlighting the role of innovative thinking and insight in scaling one’s capabilities.
Further along the path, gold represents wealth, prestige, success, and high value, emphasizing the rewards that come with persistence and dedication. Blue symbolizes trust, stability, intelligence, and professionalism, qualities that are essential for sustainable growth in both personal life and business. Black stands for authority, luxury, sophistication, and strength, reflecting maturity and leadership.
The final stage is represented by red, the strongest and highest point in the illustration. Red symbolizes power, urgency, passion, and attention. At this peak stands a trophy, representing achievement and recognition that come after successfully scaling through all stages of growth.
To further illustrate the momentum of scalability, a character rides an upward-curving arrow, symbolizing progress, ambition, and forward movement. The arrow visually connects the steps, reinforcing the idea that growth is continuous and fueled by determination.
Overall, the design communicates that scalability is not an overnight achievement but a structured progression built on motivation, values, creativity, discipline, and resilience. Each stage strengthens the individual or organization, making it possible to grow higher and stronger. The artwork celebrates the idea that with intentional steps and the right mindset, anyone can scale their life, ideas, or business toward success.

item.title: Lexx Abbey #62
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: Background Story/Motivation: The design is motivated by our reality; living/existing in a time when the world is trying to restore her old conservative and somewhat flawed values while evolving into the modern era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Mbonobong Eyo #29
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
This artwork was born from a period of personal reflection and emotional awareness. Recently, I found myself experiencing a range of emotions (tiredness, moments of happiness, sadness, confusion and even anger/pain. Despite all these internal shifts, life continued as usual. I still showed up, worked, interacted, and contributed, just like everyone else.
That realization led me to a simple but powerful thought: this is not just my experience, it is the reality of many people.
In everyday work environments, especially in co-working spaces, individuals come together to achieve shared goals. On the surface, everything appears structured, calm, and productive. However, beneath that surface, each person carries their own internal struggles, emotions, and silent battles.
This idea became the foundation of the artwork.
The piece presents a group of individuals seated together at a shared table, symbolizing unity, collaboration, and collective purpose. Their forms are abstract and faceless, intentionally simplified to remove identity and allow viewers to project themselves into the scene. Each figure represents a different emotional state; tiredness, genuine joy, sadness, confusion, and anger/pain.
While they all appear to be working together externally, their internal realities are revealed through exaggerated back drops behind them. These back drops serve as a metaphor for hidden emotions, the parts of ourselves we often conceal in professional or social settings. In this contrast between appearance and backdrop , the artwork highlights the duality of human experience: what is seen versus what is felt.
The choice of a minimal, abstract, and slightly surreal style was deliberate. By reducing detail and focusing on form, composition, and emotion, the artwork emphasizes feeling over realism. It invites viewers to pause and reflect, not just on the scene, but on their own experiences within shared spaces.
Ultimately, this piece explores the idea that co-working is not just about collaboration of tasks, but coexistence of emotions. It is about how people, despite carrying different internal worlds, still come together, function, and move forward.

item.title: Mohammed Quadri #58
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
I'm driven by the belief that the best designs and artworks are the ones that feel like a found object, something that was discovered like a lost treasure. My work is a constant conversation between my love for portrait drawing and my background in graphic design, fusing the two to create a semi abstract, sci-fi, neo brutalist style and vibrant artwork.
The portrait drawing is the second installment in my charcoal art series called Black Sapphire, it is created by capturing African beauties in their natural look, unfiltered and pure just like a Sapphire.

item.title: Nash Abraham - Submission
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
“Still Winning” was born in a season where everything felt uncertain. It came from a place of pressure, quiet battles, and moments where showing up each day felt heavier than usual. There were no loud victories at the time — just endurance, faith, and the decision to keep going even when it didn’t feel like progress was being made.
This piece is not about success in the traditional sense. It is about survival. It is about choosing to rise when it would have been easier to stay down. It reflects the reality that sometimes, winning is not in the applause or the results, but in the strength to continue.
The visual language of the artwork captures tension and persistence — the weight of life, but also the refusal to break under it. Every element is intentional, representing the silent battles people fight daily, the ones no one sees. At its core, “Still Winning” is a message of hope.
Hope is not always loud or obvious. Sometimes, hope looks like waking up and trying again. Sometimes, it looks like holding on when everything suggests letting go. It is the quiet belief that things can still change, that light can still break through, and that your story is not over.
This piece is for anyone in that space — still pushing, still believing, still standing. You are still winning.

item.title: Noel Peters #19
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: "My design revolves around the concept of ideas, showing how they form in the mind. I wanted to depict that "aha!" moment. I used a vertical orientation and an upward-facing shoe to create a sense of momentum. It’s about the aspirational feeling of "stepping up" into a new idea. The result is this piece—titled IDEAS."

item.title: Obong-Essiet Uko #49
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: My design mirrors the Stack and Huste community. Stack and Hustle is the go-to hub for any tech enthusiast who value what a true community offers. From support to newbies who are just starting out their tech journey, to supplying the necessary manpower for innovators who are developing the next big application, Stack and Hustle is that spot!

item.title: Raluchukwu Ojekwe #1
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: Para for me represent a human that is startled as the was he or she was being spoken at, leading the individual to respond in bawling tone Soul alive represents a human dancing through the spiral of life

item.title: Samuel Obiomaeze #16
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
Beyond the Noise
Beyond the Noise was inspired by my reflections on the future and the growing conversations around creativity in an increasingly technological world. The piece presents a cyberpunk-inspired cityscape imagined as a glimpse into what urban environments might feel like as technology continues to evolve and shape the spaces around us.
The work was developed through a careful digital photo manipulation process, where multiple visual elements were intentionally combined and refined to construct a single cohesive environment. Each component was thoughtfully arranged to build depth, atmosphere, and movement within the scene, allowing the city to feel alive while still leaving space for quiet human presence within it.
The title Beyond the Noise speaks to moving past the overwhelming opinions and predictions that often surround the future of creative work. Rather than focusing on those voices, the piece reflects the importance of continuing to imagine, experiment, and create through individual vision and craftsmanship.
Ultimately, the work represents a personal interpretation of possibility, an imagined future shaped by technology, but still driven by human perspective and creative intention.

item.title: Susan Solomon #15
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
This piece explores a divided digital ecosystem where two contrasting environments exist within the same framework. On one side, the Finger Monsterrs appear in muted greys and darker tones, reflecting the structured and mechanical side of digital infrastructure. In contrast, the other half features vibrant, colourful characters that represent creativity, expression, and the playful possibilities of the digital world.
Motherboard patterns and circuit lines run through the background, visually linking both sides. These elements symbolize the systems that power and connect digital spaces, suggesting that technology is not only built on rigid structures but also supports imagination, identity, and human expression.

item.title: Uchechukwu Gabriel #25
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: I created this piece from something I noticed in myself before I even saw it in the world around me. We live in a time where consuming information from multiple places at once has become normal. Not just normal, almost natural. You wake up and scroll, switch apps, check notifications, jump from one platform to another without even thinking. It feels effortless. It feels harmless. It even feels comfortable. That’s where this piece comes from. The central character represents society or maybe just me, or you lying back, relaxed, smiling, completely immersed in a stream of information. There’s no resistance. No awareness of pressure. Just passive consumption. And that’s the most unsettling part… because nothing looks wrong from the outside. But above him, there’s overload. Too much information, too many inputs, too many signals fighting to be seen. And below him, the truth reveals itself. The smaller figures represent different social platforms, each one fighting, shouting, competing for attention. They aren’t peaceful. They aren’t balanced. They are aggressive, desperate, and constantly pulling. Yet the character doesn’t react. He doesn’t even notice. That contrast is intentional. The environment, the trees, the calm setting, represents how this behavior has blended into our everyday lives. It’s no longer something we question. It’s part of the landscape now. Something that feels as natural as sitting under a tree. But it’s not. This piece is about that quiet contradiction: Being overwhelmed… but feeling fine. Being consumed… but believing you’re in control. Being surrounded by noise… yet smiling through it. That is what I call The Comfort of Overload.

item.title: Yemi Adediran #11
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
The idea was to build a Bull Campaign for Leveraged, inspired by the raw energy of a bull market: aggressive, unstoppable, and always charging forward.
At the center of it all is the Leveraged Metal Card, not just a card, but an Elite Trader Access Pass. Reserved. Earned. Never given.
The creative direction leans into dominance and prestige, sharp visuals, metallic finishes, and a tone that speaks to traders who don’t follow the market. They move it. This isn’t for everyone. It’s for the top performers. The risk-takers. The ones who understand that in a bull market, hesitation is loss, and momentum is everything.
To amplify anticipation, the campaign is supported by a short, high-energy countdown hype series for social media, building tension, driving curiosity, and signaling that something powerful is coming.

item.title: Gbolahan Gbodiyan #69
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded:
Our History explores the theme of layers by presenting Nigeria as a nation built from accumulated moments, voices, and achievements. The artwork transforms Zuma Rock into a symbolic national monument, featuring the carved faces of Obafemi Awolowo, Fela Kuti, Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala, and Wole Soyinka; figures who represent different layers of Nigeria’s political, cultural, intellectual, and artistic history.
Around them are visual echoes of defining national moments, from Olympic triumphs and the global recognition of Agbani Darego to the civic movement of the End SARS protests. Together, these scenes form a layered landscape of memory, showing that Nigeria’s identity is shaped not by a single event or person, but by the many experiences that continue to build upon one another.

item.title: James Ayoola #71
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: "Futuristic Vehicles" The inspiration behind my illustration comes part from an idea of " what if" and a blend of mechanical possibilty. Just like the movie transformers, i imagined the keke being able to achieve that level of futuristic engineering ...

item.title: Navigation
item.content:encoded: <!-- wp:page-list /-->

item.title: Navigation
item.dc:creator: stackandhustle
item.content:encoded: <!-- wp:page-list /-->

item.title: Chidinma Promise Ndukwe #43
item.dc:creator: Majid
item.content:encoded: This design is born from my passion to break into the UI design world. I'm driven by the awe I feel when I see others' work, and I hope my design sparks that same amazement in others .
